senses.definition: to impose a difficult or heavy responsibility, obligation, or hardship on an individual
to make a phone call to someone

examples.x: I'll place a call to the client to discuss the project updates.
We should place a call to the office to let them know we'll be late.
I'll place a call to the doctor's office to schedule an appointment.

word_list.pronunciation: plˈeɪs ɐ dɪmˈænd ˌɑːn ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to require a certain level of performance, effort, or output from someone or something to meet a specific objective or standard

examples.x: The company's expansion plans will place a demand on their IT department to upgrade and scale their systems accordingly.
The charity event placed a demand on volunteers who generously offered their time and assistance.

senses.definition: to give a sum of money as a prepayment or security to secure a reservation or purchase

examples.x: To reserve a table at the restaurant, you may need to place a deposit for special occasions.
To reserve the event venue, they had to place a deposit in advance.
He placed a deposit on the car he intends to purchase and will pay the balance later.
Customers often place a deposit on expensive jewelry before completing the purchase.
Many landlords require tenants to place a deposit equal to one month's rent.

senses.definition: to direct attention toward a specific topic, aspect, or issue, making it a central point of consideration

examples.x: The new policy would place a focus on environmental sustainability and conservation.
The seminar aims to place a focus on the importance of mental health in the workplace.
By hosting this event, the organization hopes to place a focus on community engagement and social responsibility.
The conference will place a focus on innovation and technological advancements in the industry.

word_list.pronunciation: plˈeɪs ɐ ɹɪstɹˈɪkʃən ɔːɹ lˈɪmɪt ˌɑːn ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to set a boundary or rule that prevents or controls certain actions, behaviors, or access

examples.x: The company placed a restriction on overtime work to maintain a healthy work-life balance for employees.

senses.definition: to determine or assign a specific worth or significance to something

examples.x: Home appraisers place a value on properties to determine their market worth.
It's important to place a value on family time and create cherished memories.
Businesses place a value on customer feedback to improve their products and services.
In a job interview, employers place a value on relevant skills and experience.

senses.definition: to give something special importance or attention

examples.x: The environmental organization places an emphasis on sustainability and conservation efforts.
The marketing campaign places an emphasis on the product's unique features and benefits.

senses.definition: to request the purchase of goods or services from a business or supplier

examples.x: She decided to place an order for a new smartphone online.
The company placed an order for office supplies to restock their inventory.
Customers can place an order for custom-made furniture to suit their preferences.
It's convenient to place an order for groceries through a mobile app and have them delivered to your doorstep.
